lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Greetings, adventurer. Welcome to the Myths' Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: How are things progressing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm yet to discover anything.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well return to us once you do. If you help us fill our Library of Knowledge, we shall grant you access to the guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the origin of Elvarg. I've found some old doors on a ruined island, but they're locked. I think something important lies behind them.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well keep at it. If you help us fill our Library of Knowledge, we shall grant you access to the guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the origin of Elvarg. I've found some old doors on a ruined island, but they're locked. I'm trying to find the key.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well keep at it. If you help us fill our Library of Knowledge, we shall grant you access to the guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm investigating the origin of Elvarg. I've found an ancient vault beneath a ruined island. I think it contains something important.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well you'd better investigate it then. If you help us fill our Library of Knowledge, we shall grant you access to the guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was investigating the origin of Elvarg but I accidently released a dragonkin!

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Well that can't be good. You'd better sort that out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Greetings adventurer. Welcome to the Myths' Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I gain access to the Guild?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Only the most worthy may access our guild and I'm afraid you are not quite there yet. Go out and hone your skills. Then you can return to us.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Only the most worthy are allowed access into the Myths' Guild. If you wish to enter, you'll need to prove yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I do that?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: As time passes, that which was once fact, becomes myth. Some tales are just that, tales. However, some tales are something more.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Here at the Myths' Guild, we live to tell the difference between the two. If you wish to join us, you must do the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what myth would you like me to investigate?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: For as long as history remembers, dragons have roamed these lands, but despite how long they've been here, much of their history remains hidden.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: There are tales of course, but tales are not enough for us. We want you to discover the truth. Where did the dragons come from? Why are they here? Who do they serve?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Answer these questions for us. Help us fill our Library of Knowledge. In return, we will grant you access to the guild and you will reap the rewards within.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: So, do you feel up for the challenge?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. Where should I begin?

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Head to Karamja Wines, Spirits, and Beers at Musa Point. There you'll find Dallas Jones. We've heard that he is investigating the destruction of Crandor. This seems like a good place to start.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. This is too much for me.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: As you wish. If you change your mind, you know where we are.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'm [player name]. Are you Dallas Jones?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I am. Pleased to meet you. I'm assuming you're here because of my advert?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... yes?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Excellent! Let me run you through the details.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: We're to head to Elvarg's lair beneath Crandor. There we will begin a research project on Elvarg and her attack on the island.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I trust you know the tale of Elvarg?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I was the one who killed her in the first place.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: You're the Hero of Crandor? Amazing!

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I take it you know her story very well then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Thirty years ago, an adventurer descended into the caves beneath Crandor. There he discovered the dragon Elvarg.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Elvarg made short work of the adventurer. Once she was finished with him, she emerged from the caves and laid waste to the island.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: For the next thirty years, Elvarg remained on Crandor until she was finally defeated, by you.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Now that Elvarg is dead, we have the opportunity to perform research on the island and investigate her attack.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Meet me in Elvarg's Lair beneath Crandor when you're ready to begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. See you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I know it well.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Wonderful. I won't waste your time with it then.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Now that Elvarg is dead, we have the opportunity to perform research on the island and investigate her attack.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Meet me in Elvarg's Lair beneath Crandor when you're ready to begin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. See you there.

sections.title: Talking to Dallas in Elvarg's Lair

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about the investigation.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found a strange wall that looks like it might have something behind it. We might be able to mine through it.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Good work. I'm afraid you'll need to be the one to do the mining. My back isn't what it once was.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Not much to tell really. My name is Dallas Jones and I'm a researcher for the Varrock Museum. Many of the researchers have an area of expertise. Mine is dragons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why dragons?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: My father used to live on Crandor before it was destroyed. He was one of the many who were killed by Elvarg.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: His death left me with a great interest in dragons and I've spent the last fifteen years trying to learn more about them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks, Dallas.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Dallas. So what exactly are we researching here?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Elvarg's attack on Crandor is a bit of an anomaly. We're here to work out why.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What made it an anomaly?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Elvarg is very unusual in that she is one of the few dragons to live alone. Most of her kind live in large groups.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Not only that, she also displayed far greater aggression than most other dragons by directly attacking Crandor.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But aren't all dragons aggressive?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Not like Elvarg was. Most dragons will only attack those who enter their territory. Elvarg strayed well outside of hers when she attacked the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Where should we start?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: This is where Elvarg made her home. There must be something here to point us in the right direction. Have a look around and see what you can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about the investigation.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've mined through a strange wall and found a tunnel behind it.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I can see that. We could well be on to something here. You go through the tunnel and see if it's safe. I'll be right behind you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about the investigation.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I just can't believe it. This place may not just hold the secrets of Elvarg, but of the whole dragon race!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll look around and see what I can find.

sections.title: Talking to Dallas after killing the spawn

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: What was that creature?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have no idea.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: How strange. We should keep looking around.

sections.title: Talking to Dallas after inspecting the mural

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found an old mural. It has lots of dragons and fungi on it along with some strange writing.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: What did it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know. It looked like a different language. It had odd words on it like 'Lithkren'.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Lithkren? That name sounds familiar. Where have I seen it before?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Of course! Fossil Island! I was there recently as part of the research team investigating the differences between wyverns and dragons.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: There's an old settlement on the island with similar architecture to this place. It contains lots of books, many written in a strange language. One of the books mentioned a place called Lithkren.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, now that you mention it, I think I may have read that book as well.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well that just confirms it! Not to mention that Fossil Island is also coated in various types of fungi. It must be connected!

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Fossil Island is also coated in various types of fungi. It must be connected!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In that case, we should probably go to Fossil Island.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I agree. Head to the old settlement on the north side of the island. I'll meet you there once I've finished up here.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: It looks like we've found everything here. We should move on to Fossil Island. I'll meet you at the old settlement on the north side of the island.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Goodness me! What is this place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks like some sort of laboratory.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Are the tales true? Could this mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mean what?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: There are ancient tales that tell of the origin of the dragons. They say they were created by a powerful race of creatures known as the dragonkin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dragonkin? I've heard of them.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Yes, you likely will have. We know for a fact that the dragonkin existed. They terrorised the world for many years during the early Fourth Age.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: The thing is, despite their similar names, no actual connection between the dragonkin and the dragons has ever been proven.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe we'll be the ones to prove it.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: We might just be. We should look around and see what we can uncover.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Dallas.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: So while I was waiting for you, I had a look through these books again. One of them mentions a nearby island called Lithkren. Thing is, there are lots of islands around here. It doesn't say which one it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do we know which island to go to?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Have a look at this grid on the floor. I think it might actually be a map. If you look at that tile in the middle, it looks like part of this island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... So this map could lead us to Lithkren. Most of the pieces are missing though.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: That they are. However, I had a look around and I managed to find some of the pieces.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: By my calculations, there are 24 pieces left to find. I suspect they'll either be in this building or very close by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! I'll have a look around and see if I can find them.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Good luck. Once you have all of the pieces, bring them here and we'll see about putting this map together.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which book was it?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Check the pile behind the ladder. You should find it there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do we know which island to go to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about the investigation.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Have you managed to find those map pieces yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well keep looking. We have [amount] pieces left to find. I suspect they'll either be in this building or very close by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I have some here.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Great. I'll look after them for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that all of them?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Not yet. We have [amount] pieces left to find. I suspect they'll either be in this building or very close by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that's all of them.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: It looks like it is. Good job.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Now then, shall we see about finishing this map?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Fair enough. Let me know when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing again?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: We'll need a boat to get to Lithkren. Jardric should be able to help us. See if you can find him. He should be somewhere on the island.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I do believe you've got it! Yes, we should be able to find Lithkren now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But which island is it?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: There's a strange symbol on the map in two places. One marks this building and the other marks an island. I bet that's Lithkren.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you might be right. So now that we know where the island is, we need to find a way there. Do you think the museum can help us?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: The museum? No that won't work. Any requisition of museum resources requires a mountain of paperwork and we simply don't have time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't we?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Of course not! Besides, I've spent years working towards this and I'm tired of waiting. It's time to discover the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, alright. So what do we do instead?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: We'll need a boat. Find a dwarf called Jardric. He should be somewhere on the island. He's an old friend who owes me a favour. He should be able to get a boat for us.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: While you're doing that, I'm going to take a copy of this map.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Hello again. How's the boat coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need again?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: You'll need a saw, a hammer, eight oak planks, ten swamp paste and 12 nails of any kind. The best place to build the boat will be on the west side of the island, near the mushroom forest.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Jardric?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: That I am. Former boatman and now a researcher for Keldagrim. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working with Dallas Jones on a research project. We're investigating the destruction of Crandor. We need a boat to get to one of the nearby islands. Dallas said you could help us.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Did he now? Looking to cash in on that favour I bet. I'm afraid I can't help you. All the boats around here are owned by the museum and they won't just let you take them.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Of course, Dallas works with the museum and he could easily put in a request for one, but he's not a fan of waiting around...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said as much. So what can we do?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Well I suppose you could always build your own. If the island isn't far you'll only need a small rowboat.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Mind you, you'll want to be quite good at building things, unless you fancy a trip to the bottom of the ocean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not actually too bad when it comes to construction. What will I need?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Let's see... I'd say eight oak planks, ten swamp paste and 12 nails of any kind should do it. Obviously you'll need a hammer and a saw too.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: You'll probably be wanting someone to look after the boat as well. I guess I can do that. But this means that Dallas and I are even.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: There should be a good spot to build the boat on the west side of the island, near the mushroom forest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thanks for the help.

sections.title: Talking to Jardric at the roawboat space

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Hello again. This should be a good place to build the boat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need again?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: You'll need a saw, a hammer, eight oak planks, ten swamp paste and 12 nails of any kind.

sections.title: Talking to Dallas at the roawboat space

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the boat all finished.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: And not a bad boat either. Good job.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: This is it. This is our chance to finally find some answers. To finally find out the truth behind Elvarg. Are you ready to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Look at this place! I'm going to explore and see what I can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Someone's in a hurry. I suppose I should look around as well.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: And I guess I'll stay here and look after the boat. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well don't take too long. We've had to wait years for this. I don't want to wait much longer.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Hello again. How's the boat coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well talk to Jardric if you need some help. He'll know what to do.

sections.title: Talking to Dallas at the Ancient Doors

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Me neither.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well those are some big doors.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Look at them. Look at the markings on them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dragons?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Exactly. This is it. This has to be the place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall we go in?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: We can't. I already tried, but the doors are sealed. There's a circular indentation in the middle. Maybe it's a keyhole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well a keyhole isn't much good if there isn't a key. We should look around and see if there are any clues nearby.

sections.title: Talking to Dallas after finding Aivas' diary

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Hello. Have you found that cat yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet I'm afraid. He likes to move around a lot.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well keep at it. We need to get through these doors.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found an old diary nearby but I haven't read it yet.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well read it and see what it says. It could well contain the information we need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found an old diary nearby but I haven't read it yet.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well read it and see what it says. It could well contain the information we need.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found an old diary nearby. It mentioned a battle that took place here involving Robert the Strong.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Robert the Strong? He spent most of his life fighting the dragonkin. Does this mean it's true? Did they really create the dragons?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I guess we'll never know though. Robert the Strong is gone. We've reached a dead end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not necessarily. Robert the Strong might not be as dead as you think.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Robert the Strong is still alive. He's been reincarnated as a cat called Bob.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Just think once before you speak please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm serious! Why would I lie about this?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well I'm definitely not convinced, but let's say you're telling the truth... how can a cat help us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have an amulet that lets me talk to cats. I can use it to ask Bob what he knows.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: This is all very confusing. You go find this cat of yours. I need to sit down and clear my head of all this weirdness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well. I shall return once I've learnt more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Bob!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. I'm on my way to Sophanem to see the Sphinx.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! I'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Bob!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How's things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not bad. I was wondering if you could help me with something.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I guess that depends what it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you know how you're actually Robert the Strong?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: So you and the Sphinx tell me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Long story, I'll keep it brief. I found an old dungeon with a large set of doors in it. There was a skeleton nearby with a diary that mentioned Robert the Strong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping you could use your memories of Robert the Strong to help me open the doors.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: But I don't have any memories of Robert the Strong. It was only when the Sphinx hypnotised me that we learnt who I really was and I don't even remember that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... This is problematic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe the Sphinx can help us again.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well it worked last time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That it did. Bob, would you mind meeting me in Sophanem?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well I suppose I don't have anything better to do. Not unless Unferth has forgotten where he lives again.

lines.speaker: Neite
lines.text: Ahem.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Oh fudge. Give me one moment, [player name].

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well that all sounds great to me, [player name]. I'll meet you in Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Bob.

lines.speaker: Neite
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Neite.

lines.speaker: Neite
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's Bob?

lines.speaker: Neite
lines.text: He's meant to be with you. You haven't lost him have you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Neite
lines.text: Well keep an eye on him for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably wear my catspeak amulet before talking to Bob and the Sphinx.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Hello, human. Bob was just telling me about your dungeon.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: That's right. Before we get into that though, about that thing I mentioned...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thing?

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Ah yes. That amulet of yours is very useful, but it has limitations. I can use my power to grant you the ability to talk to cats even when you're not wearing it.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Very few humans are ever given this power, but you've proven yourself to be a friend to feline kind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm very honoured.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: Excellent. Prepare yourself!

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: All done. Now, what is it you need my help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We were hoping you could hypnotise Bob again so we can learn more about his past.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: What is it you wish to learn?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's about that dungeon. I believe that it was once visited by Robert the Strong. I want to know what he did while he was there.

lines.speaker: Sphinx
lines.text: I'm afraid I will be of little help here. All I can do is hypnotise Bob. I can't enter his mind and choose which memory to view.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Enter his mind...

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: What are you thinking, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I once entered the mind of a guy called Cyrisus to confront some personal issues of his. The Oneiromancer on Lunar Isle helped me do it. Maybe she can help us now.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Okay, I'll join you there. That's going to be a long walk on these paws.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: More walking? Why don't we ever get a nice easy teleport for these things?

sections.title: Talking to Bob before leaving Sophanem

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I'm not ready to set out yet. You go on ahead. I'll meet you on Lunar Isle and we'll ask the Oneiromancer to help us.

sections.title: Talking to Bob on Lunar Isle
Talking to Bob again before making the dream potion

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You should get to work on making that potion.

sections.title: Talking to Bob after making the dream potion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, Bob! I've finished the Potion of Shared Dreaming.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Good stuff. Take it to the brazier in the middle of town and we can begin the dream.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. The Oneiromancer and I were just discussing what our perfect Sunday would be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't know you two knew each other. Also, how can you speak to cats?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You know it's really not that hard. You just need a bit of magic on your side. Anyway, Bob and I go way back.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Seems like everyone knows everyone in these parts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh... small world.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: So what was it you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to unlock Bob's memories from his old life as Robert the Strong. We were hoping that we could use a shared dream.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Well we've never actually done a shared dream between a human and a cat before, but it shouldn't be any different. You'll just need another Potion of Shared Dreaming.

lines.speaker: Cat
lines.text: Should we be worried here?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Would you like me to remind you how they are made?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: I'll give you a vial. You'll need to fill it with water, followed by one ground astral rune and some goutweed.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Once you've done that, light the brazier in the middle of town. When you're ready to begin your dream, just use the potion on the brazier.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You'll need to make some room in your inventory for this vial. Come back to me when you have.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Here's a vial for your potion. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You'll need to make some room in your inventory for this vial. Come back to me when you have.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Here's a vial for your potion. Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to the Oneiromancer before making the dream potion
Talking to her after making the dream potion

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've made the Potion of Shared Dreaming.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: In that case you should take it to the brazier in the middle of town.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Hello there. What do you want to talk about?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: How are things going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Take the vial I gave you and fill it with water. After that, add one ground astral rune and some goutweed.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: You'll need to make some room in your inventory for this vial. Come back to me when you have.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
