sections.title: Talking to Bob by the Ceremonial Brazier

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, Bob! I've finished the Potion of Shared Dreaming.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Good stuff. Light the brazier then add the potion.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Good stuff. Add the potion to the brazier and we can begin.

sections.title: Using the potion on the brazier

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. Shall we go back into the dream again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ready, Bob?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Ready when you are, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be doing again?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Apparently you need to defeat the guardian on the other side of this barrier. Sounds odd to me, but what do I know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I meant to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: Pass through the barrier and defeat the guardian. That should restore Bob's lost memories.

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: Well one thing's for sure, I'm definitely Not Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Okay... so who are you then?

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: I just told you, I'm Not Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Well this is confusing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These dreams usually are.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: So Not Bob, why are you here?

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: You tell me. It's your mind we're in right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're here to recover Bob's lost memories from his life as Robert the Strong. Do you think you can help us?

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: I suppose I can. This barrier represents the divide between Bob the Cat and Robert the Strong. The memories you seek are right here, on the other side of the barrier.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: So all we need to do is go through the barrier?

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: Going through the barrier will give you access to Robert's memories, but only while you're in the dream. As soon as you leave, you'll forget them again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how can we permanently unlock them?

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: Destroying the barrier of course. Head through the barrier and defeat the guardian. That should do the trick.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Defeat the guardian? I'm no good at combat... I'm a cat.

lines.speaker: Not Bob
lines.text: Good job this is a shared dream then. It doesn't have to be you that defeats him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like it's up to me then. Wish me luck.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Good luck, [player name].

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, Bob. Could you remind me where those key pieces are?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: The key was split into four different pieces. Camorra took one piece, Tristan took another and I took the final two.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Camorra boarded a ship for lands to the far west, never to be seen again. She took her key piece with her.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Tristan passed his piece on to his descendants. They lived in the city of Saranthium, where Varrock now stands. Perhaps the Varrock Library has some record of them.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I hid one of my pieces in the most unlikely of places, an abandoned dragonkin fortress. You'll find the fortress on a snow covered island, north west of Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: The final piece I took to Karamja. I hid it in an ancient temple on the south coast of the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I remember! Lithkren! The dragonkin! The vault!

lines.speaker: Dragonkin
lines.text: Robert! I should have known that this would be your doing.

lines.speaker: Robert the Strong
lines.text: It's over dragonkin. The island is ours. You have lost.

lines.speaker: Dragonkin
lines.text: Lost? This is no more than a minor setback. The work we have done here will see the end of your kind. You are too late to stop it.

lines.speaker: Aivas
lines.text: And what work might that be?

lines.speaker: Dragonkin
lines.text: Our new weapon. One that will bring a swift end to humanity. I'd love to be able to show you, but I'm afraid I have more pressing matters.

lines.speaker: Camorra
lines.text: Like dying perhaps?

lines.speaker: Dragonkin
lines.text: You'd like that, wouldn't you? Alas, I'm afraid that I won't be the one that dies today.

lines.speaker: Tristan
lines.text: We'll see about that.

lines.speaker: Dragonkin
lines.text: Yes we will, for this is where it begins.

lines.speaker: Robert the Strong
lines.text: No. This is where it ends.

lines.speaker: Tristan
lines.text: Aivas!

lines.speaker: Camorra
lines.text: You'll pay for that!

lines.speaker: Tristan
lines.text: What is that?

lines.speaker: Robert the Strong
lines.text: Looks like some sort of key. For these doors I assume.

lines.speaker: Camorra
lines.text: Should we open them?

lines.speaker: Robert the Strong
lines.text: No. That dragonkin mentioned a weapon. One that could destroy us all. Better something like that remains hidden... for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing, Bob?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I've got a lot to take in and understand, but I'm okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you have all of your memories back now?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes and no. I now have my old memories again, but there are so many of them. I can't quite piece them all together yet. I need time to properly work things out.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: However, one thing that I do remember clearly is that battle. I also remember what happened next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what did happen?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: After the battle, myself, Tristan and Camorra split the key into four pieces. We vowed to ensure that the dragonkin would never recover them, and to only return once the war was won.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you never did?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No. In the end, we went our seperate ways. Of the four pieces, Camorra took one, Tristan took another and I took the final two.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Camorra eventually boarded a ship for lands to the far west, never to be seen again. She took her key piece with her.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Tristan passed his piece on to his descendants. They lived in the city of Saranthium, where Varrock now stands. Perhaps the Varrock Library has some record of them.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I hid one of my pieces in the most unlikely of places, an abandoned dragonkin fortress. You'll find the fortress on a snow covered island, north west of Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: The final piece I took to Karamja. I hid it in an ancient temple on the south coast of the island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like I have a fair amount of work to do. Thanks, Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: With the dragonkin long gone, the vault should be safe to open. That said, you should still be cautious, [player name].

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: The scars of our war with the dragonkin remain to this day, and the weapon in that vault could still be very dangerous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll be careful.

sections.title: Talking to him before finding the book

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Have you found that book yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I know it's around here somewhere. It's red if I remember right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I was wondering if you could help me with something?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for information on a Fourth Age hero. His name was Tristan and he fought alongside Robert the Strong.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah, you must mean Tristan Corvo. I can't remember anyone ever asking about him before. Most prefer to hear about the bigger heroes like Arrav or Robert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you know about Tristan?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Oh yes. What was it you wanted to learn?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to learn about his descendants. I know they lived in this area.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well we do have census records which date back to when the city was still called Avarrocka, but I'm afraid we don't have anything from before that time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... It might still be worth a look. Where can I find this information?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I have a book around here somewhere with all of the details. Why don't you see if you can find it? It's red if I remember right.

sections.title: Talking to Reldo with the book

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Did you have any luck finding the Corvo family?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Port Phasmatys might be a good place to look. It's where most of the Morytania settlers wound up.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Have you found that book yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it here.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Wonderful! Let's have a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what does the book say?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Okay let's see... The Corvo family... Here we are! According to this, there was a family by the name of Corvo living in Avarrocka for most of the Fourth Age.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: The last entry is from the year 1770 of the Fourth Age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to them?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Impossible to say for sure, but it was around that time that many people left Misthalin to settle in Morytania. It's possible that the Corvo family was among them.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I'm afraid that's all the help I can provide. Port Phasmatys is where most of the Morytania settlers wound up. Perhaps you'll find what you're looking for there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks Reldo.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Do you know anyone with the name Corvo?

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Sarah Corvo? She lives in the house next to the bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: You again. You're not still after that key piece are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nope. Just stopping by to say hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that was great. See you later.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Do you know anyone with the name Corvo?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Of course. I'm Sarah Corvo. Not many people interested in visiting me these days. What brings you here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm on the hunt for the piece of a key. It was once in the possession of your ancestor, Tristan Corvo.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I see. I'm afraid I can't help you. The key piece is gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gone?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Yes gone. When my father passed the key piece down to me, he told me that I must keep it safe no matter what. When the vampyres came to Port Phasmatys, I buried it out in the swamps to stop them getting it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how do I get it back?

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: You don't. The key piece is gone and it's safer that way. I never liked looking after that red lump of metal anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I think I might know just the scientist to help me out with this. Time for me to visit Draynor Manor.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, nothing. I'd best be off. Thank you for your time.

sections.title: Talking to her again before making the orb

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hello again. Are you after another device, or some information, or would you like to see my goods for sale?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with a key piece.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Have you crafted that orb yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need again?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You adventurers are so forgetful. You'll need one cut dragonstone and two molten glass. Use a glassblowing pipe to craft a glass orb with the dragonstone in the centre. When you're done, bring it back here.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hello again. Are you after another device, or some information, or would you like to see my goods for sale?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with a key piece.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: A key piece?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I'm looking for the piece of a key made out of red metal. I know it's buried somewhere in Mort Myre Swamp, but I don't know exactly where. I was thinking...

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You were thinking that because I made you a metal attracting device before that I'd be able to do it again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... yeah, pretty much.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Should be doable. Will need to be quite different to your device though. Much greater distance and a different type of metal. You said it was red, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes it's...

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Must be orikalkum. Dragon as you adventuring lot call it. Quite rare. Should make things easier. Now let me think about this... If we just... hmm... yes... perfect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Go get one cut dragonstone and two molten glass. Use a glassblowing pipe to craft a glass orb with the dragonstone in the centre. Once you have it, bring it to me. Any questions? Good. Bye.

sections.title: Talking to Ava after making the orb
Talking to her again after receiving the orb

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hello again. Are you after another device, or some information, or would you like to see my goods for sale?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with a key piece.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: I already made you a locator orb. What else do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does it work?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: The orb will pull you in the direction of the key piece, but be careful with it. It's a little unstable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't get mad.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: You lost it, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Well luckily for you, I made a spare. Please try to look after this one.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hello again. Are you after another device, or some information, or would you like to see my goods for sale?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Have you crafted that orb yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have indeed.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hand it over then.

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Excellent. Now just a few small adjustments and...

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Here you go. You can use this to track down that key piece of yours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! How does it work?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: The orb will pull you in the direction of the key piece, but be careful with it. It's a little unstable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awesome! That's just... Wait... what do you mean unstable?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Nothing to get worried about. It might just sting a bit when you use it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... great? Anyway, before I go I was wondering...

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: If I could upgrade that device of yours?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well now that you mention it...

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Hmm... Might be possible. The chicken is the weakness at the moment. If you find something with a bit more kick, I might be able to upgrade it further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bit more kick?

lines.speaker: Ava
lines.text: Yes. Something a bit stronger. A bit more undead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... Thanks for the help Ava. See you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Good day, Bwana. The Kharazi jungle is especially beautiful today, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for an ancient temple.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Did you try that dungeon in the south east corner of the jungle, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't. I'll look there. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: Good day, Bwana. The Kharazi jungle is especially beautiful today, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for an ancient temple.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: A temple? There's an old dungeon in the south east corner of the jungle. Could this be what you seek, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It may well be. Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Gujuo
lines.text: I must warn you, Bwana, that place is dangous. Many of our people have entered and never returned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It can never be easy can it? Okay, I'll be careful. Thanks again.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings, [Fremennik name]. Have you destroyed that beast yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well if you speak to Torfinn on the docks he will take you to Ungael. Good luck, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings, [Fremennik name]. How are things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Brundt. I wanted to ask you about something.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well ask away, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently there's an island north of here with an ancient fortress on it. Have you heard of it?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I think I know of this island. Ungael, we call it. It is a dark place, guarded by a deadly beast. Some of our finest warriors tried to take the beast down, but to no success.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do you intend to slay this beast for yourself, [Fremennik name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... yes, I think I do.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This is no easy task, [Fremennik name], but glory awaits you if you succeed.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Go see Torfinn on the docks. He can take you to Ungael. Good luck, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will he talk to me while I'm exiled?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: He should do. Just tell him I sent you.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: Good day to you, [Fremennik name]. Do you want to go to Ungael?

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: I'm not meant to be talking to you [Fremennik name]. I guess I can still take you to Ungael though if you want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: Good day to you, [Fremennik name]. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The chieftain says you can take me to Ungael.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: That I can. You should know though, Ungael is a dangerous place. It is guarded by a deadly foe, one that spits fire at its enemies.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: I shouldn't really be talking to you [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know but the Chieftain says you can take me to Ungael.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: Well if the Chieftain says it's okay then I guess I can. You should know though, Ungael is a dangerous place. It is guarded by a deadly force, one that spits fire at its enemies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Spits fire? Sounds like some sort of fire protection might be useful.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: So, [Fremennik name], are you ready to go to Ungael?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: Then we shall go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: I will be here when you are.

lines.speaker: Veos
lines.text: Hey there! Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever heard of Camorra?

lines.speaker: Veos
lines.text: I'm afraid not. Did you try the Library Historical Archive?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Veos
lines.text: I suggest you try there. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Veos
lines.text: Hey there! Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever heard of Camorra?

lines.speaker: Veos
lines.text: Camorra? What's a Camorra?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She was an ancient hero who lived about two thousand years ago. I believe she travelled here at some point.

lines.speaker: Veos
lines.text: An ancient hero? Sounds like the Library Historical Archive is where you want to be. Talk to Archeio in the Arceuus Library. He can take you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Welcome to the Library Historical Archive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know anything about Camorra?

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: I think I have heard that name before. Who is she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She was a hero who lived around two thousand years ago.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: I see. We have very few records from before the founding of Kourend. That said, perhaps Imerominia could help you? As a horologist, such ancient topics may fall within her purview.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Istoria
lines.text: Is there something else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: Hello. My name is Imerominia and I'm the library horologist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever heard of Camorra?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: I have not. Did you try talking to the Hosidius and Shayzien historians about her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find them again?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: Jonathan is the Hosidius historian. He lives west of the Hosidius General Store. Amelia is the Shayzien historian. She lives south west of the Shayzien Pub.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: Hello. My name is Imerominia and I'm the library horologist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you ever heard of Camorra?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: I can't say I have. Who is she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's a hero from around two thousand years ago. I believe she travelled to Kourend during the Dragonkin Conflicts. I was hoping to learn more about her.

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: Two thousand years ago? That's long before the founding of Kourend. I'm afraid we have very few records from those times here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. So you can't help me?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: I can't, but someone might be able to. The five cities of Kourend were founded long before the kingdom itself. Each city has their own historian who might have more information on the early days of their city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. Is there a particular city you'd recommend?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: Hosidius and Shayzien were the two cities founded during the Dragonkin Conflicts. They might be the best ones to start with. The other cities were founded much earlier, during the God Wars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find the Hosidius and Shayzien historians?

lines.speaker: Imerominia
lines.text: Jonathan is the Hosidius historian. He lives west of the Hosidius General Store. Amelia is the Shayzien historian. She lives south west of the Shayzien Pub.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Have you heard of Camorra?

lines.speaker: Jonathan
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Camorra. She was a great hero who fought in the Dragonkin Conflicts. I think she travelled to Kourend during the war. I'm trying to find out what happened to her.

lines.speaker: Jonathan
lines.text: A hero? Well she wouldn't have had much to do with Hosidius. The early Hosidius settlers were all pacificts. They'd have stayed away from people like her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well. Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. Have you heard of Camorra?

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: I'm sure we've already spoken about this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We probably have. I'm very forgetful.

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: The most likely place to find answers is the Shayzien Crypts. You'll find the entrance in the Graveyard of Heroes, west of here. Be careful, it's filled with zombies and skeletons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: Hello. Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you heard of Camorra?

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: Not that I remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well she was a great hero during the Dragonkin Conflicts. I think she travelled to Kourend before the war ended. I'm trying to find out more about her.

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: Well Shayzien was founded by those fleeing the Dragonkin Conflicts, and the city has never been short of heroes. There's a good chance she was connected to Shayzien in some way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you've never heard of her?

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: No, but that's not surprising. Much of our history was lost during Xeric's reign. However, there is somewhere you might find answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where's that?

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: The Shayzien Crypts. They were built before the kingdom even existed. Some of the oldest Shayzien heroes are buried there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let me guess... there's a catch isn't there?

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: I'm afraid so. For hundreds of years, the crypts have been overrun by the dead. We've tried clearing them out, but they just come back again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well thankfully I ain't afraid of no ghosts. Where do I start?

lines.speaker: Amelia
lines.text: They're zombies and skeletons, not ghosts. You'll find the entrance to the crypts in the Graveyard of Heroes, west of here. Be careful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for the help.

sections.title: Using all four dragon key pieces on the anvil

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello again, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's it going, Bob?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Now that I've started to regain my memories, I find myself hungry for answers. I'd like to see for myself what these doors guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well we'll be finding out very soon. I've managed to fix the key.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Let's see if it works then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've managed to repair the key.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Well no point waiting around. Let's get these doors open.

sections.title: Talking to either in the vault

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my!

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: That is one big dragon!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: This isn't right... We've made a mistake. We need to leave now!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Magnificent isn't he?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: His name is Galvek. In our tongue, it means...

lines.speaker: Dragonkin
lines.text: Fate.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You! I remember you. Zorgoth.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Hello, Robert. You've had quite the makeover.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: You should be dead.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: We dragonkin are not as flimsy as you. I'm afraid you failed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a dragonkin?

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Ah, I see you brought your friend with you. I'm surprised to see you here after all this time. Curious...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you...

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Hello there, dragonkin.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: You I don't recognise. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I'm Dallas Jones, and I came here for answers.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Is that so? Then tell me, Dallas Jones, what answers do you seek?

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: I want to know the truth about Elvarg, about Crandor.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Elvarg? Crandor? These names mean nothing to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Elvarg was a dragon who attacked the island of Crandor. We found a lab hidden in her lair. We followed the clues and they led us here, to you.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: You gave her a name? I never bothered. She was nothing.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Nothing? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: After your failed attempt on my life, I realised that we were starting to lose the war against your kind. We needed to turn the tide. We needed a new weapon.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: The creature you call Elvarg was the first. An attempt to create a being which would crush humanity once and for all. She was a failure. Too weak. Pathetic.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: We moved on. We tried various other methods, all ending in disappointment. In the end, we came here. Our largest facility yet. Here we began work on what would become our masterpiece.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: But we got here first.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: An annoying setback. When you attacked, I had this place sealed, to keep our work safe. My people were to return after the attack and release me again.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: But we killed them before they could leave.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: I suspected as much. However, it turned out quite well for me in the end. I have been sealed here for hundreds of years, alone and without disturbance. More than enough time for me to perfect our salvation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Galvek.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Yes. He has power that no other dragon has. The power to influence the minds of other dragons. Through him, an army will be raised. One that will annihilate humanity.

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Why? You attacked us. Tried to destroy us. You took away our homes, our people. Were we expected to stand by and watch?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: We were defending ourselves. You threatened to destroy us all.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Not all of us. Only a few gave in to the anger of the curse. Only a few waged war on you. But you were not willing to distinguish. And now, neither am I.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Humanity is a disease. You have just unleashed the cure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you really think we're letting you leave this place?

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: I don't think you have a choice. Let me show you...

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Awaken!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: It's moving!

lines.speaker: Dallas Jones
lines.text: Aaargh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dallas!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Meow!

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Aaargh!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: We'd better go!

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: What's happening?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Aaargh!

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: You dare!

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: The doors are unlocked! Release us!

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: That really hurt!

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: It looks like we may have a slight problem on our hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do? That dragonkin said he was going to assemble an army of dragons!

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: This is too much for just us to deal with. We're going to need some help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any suggestions?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: King Roald commands one of the largest armies in the world. He's probably a good starting point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good plan. I'll go see the King.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: And I'll get in touch with Keldagrim. Maybe they can help us.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Good luck, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Hello there. Have you spoken to the other leaders yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. Who am I meant to be speaking to again?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Talk to King [Lathas/Thoros], Chieftain Brundt and Sir Amik Varze. Tell them that they are needed in Varrock to discuss the dragon threat. Now hurry up and get it done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Hello there. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to talk about dragons.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: If you mean the dragons that have been spotted flying around, we're well aware. I have my best people investigating this and we will make sure the citizens are safe. Now, be on your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! I know what the dragons are doing.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. There's a dragonkin named Zorgoth who has managed to bring them under his control. He is forming an army which he intends to use to attack humanity!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: A dragonkin? That's preposterous. The dragonkin have been dead for hundreds of years.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know it sounds crazy, but you have to believe me! Why else would the dragons be acting so odd?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well I suppose you've been a friend to Varrock in the past. What would you ask of us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need to gather your soldiers. We have to destroy this dragon threat before it destroys us.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Gather the soldiers? My lord, do you know what this means?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Of course I know what it means. But this citizen is right, we have no choice.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: But my lord...

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Enough. This enemy is threatening us directly and we must respond.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Enough. This isn't like Morytania. There is no godly involvement here. This enemy is threatening us directly and we must respond.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I will make the necessary preparations. In the meantime, citizen, you will visit King [Lathas/Thoros], Chieftain Brundt and Sir Amik Varze. Tell them to come to Varrock to discuss the dragon threat.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Chieftain Brundt? You would ally with those barbarians?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: For Saradomin's sake! They have ships and they have soldiers! We need them on our side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure if we want King Lathas on our side.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: You as well? Put your petty disagreements aside and look at the big picture. We must all work together if we are to survive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you're right.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Now, you should get moving. Time is not on our side. While you're gone, I'll have Aeonisig reach out to some other people as well. We need all the help we can get.

sections.title: Talking to Sir Amik Varze again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Sir Amik.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello there. I will be leaving for Varrock soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Sir Amik.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Hello there. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to talk about dragons.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: I assume you mean all these dragons that have been flying around. Do you know something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do. It's not good I'm afraid. The dragons are uniting under a dragonkin named Zorgoth. He intends to use them against us.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: I thought all the dragonkin were dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid not.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: History says they are a formidable enemy. Do you have a plan to combat this threat?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Roald is forming an army to take on the dragons. He's requested your presence in Varrock to discuss this.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Very well, I shall head to Varrock. Thank you for passing on this message.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good day my lord.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: Greetings. Do you require something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wanted to talk to you about some dragons.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: We've had various reports come in about a sudden increase in dragon activity. I assume this is what you're referring to?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It is. The increase in activity is caused by a dragonkin called Zorgoth. He's forming an army of dragons to destroy humanity with.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: I see. I will ensure our forces are ready to fight back against this threat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need not fight it alone. King Roald is preparing his own men and would like you to fight with him. A meeting is to be held in Varrock to plan the defence.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: An alliance with King Roald? Interesting. We have no quarrel with Misthalin. I accept.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great. I'll see you in Varrock.

lines.speaker: King Thoros
lines.text: On behalf of everyone in Ardougne, I thank you for your help adventurer. I'm afraid I don't have time to talk, my predecessor has left me with a lot of work to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I appreciate you're busy, but I wanted to talk to you about some dragons.

lines.speaker: King Thoros
lines.text: We've had various reports come in about a sudden increase in dragon activity. I assume this is what you're referring to?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings, [Fremennik name]. Have you seen those beasts flying towards Ungael? Might be best to stay clear of that place for a while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Brundt. That's what I wanted to talk to you about.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Let me guess, you intend to take these beasts on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You are a true warrior, [Fremennik name], though I fear this may be too much even for you. You are heavily outnumbered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't plan on doing it alone. I have the support of King Roald. I was hoping you'd help as well.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Fight alongside outerlanders? That is not typically our way, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand. However, these dragons are threatening humanity as a whole. We must fight back.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well let it not be said that the Fremennik turned away from a good fight. You have our support. Maybe we'll show your outerlander friends a thing or two!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The council won't be happy. I'll tell them we're doing it to show your outerlander friends how to fight. That should convince them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Brundt. There is to be a meeting in Varrock to discuss our plan of action. It would be good if you could attend.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Pah! Outerlanders and their meetings. I will attend, but this better not take long. We have dragons to kill!

sections.title: Talking to Brundt the Chieftain again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Brundt. Are you ready for the meeting in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I shall be heading there soon, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In that case, I'll see you there.

sections.title: Talking to Bob before the meeting

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've arranged a meeting with King Roald and some other rulers. We're going to discuss how to combat the dragons.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: It's not the dragons I'm worried about, [player name]. It's Zorgoth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll beat him.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I hope you're right. You'd better head in and get this meeting done. Come talk to me once it's finished. There's something we need to discuss.

sections.title: Talking to Jardric before the meeting

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Jardric.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Hello there. While you were arranging this meeting I spoke to the Consortium in Keldagrim. I'm afraid they won't be helping us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well we still have Roald and the other rulers.

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: Indeed. Speaking of which, you should head on in. The meeting will be starting soon.

sections.title: Talking to King Roald after gathering the leaders

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Good work gathering the other leaders. The meeting will begin shortly. Head into the dining room when you are ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Greetings, your majesty.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Our armies are preparing for the assault on Ungael. Speak to Torfinn in Rellekka when you are ready, he will take you to the fleet.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Thank you all for coming. As I'm sure you are aware, we are facing one of the greatest threats ever seen in our time.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Our job here is to decide upon a solution and then enact it.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: The solution is clear. We need to take these dragons head on and destroy them.

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: Careful Lathas. I have seen the horrors of war first hand. This is not something we should merrily jump into.

lines.speaker: King Lathas
lines.text: And what would the great King Arthur have us do? Would you cower in fear while these dragons annihilate those around you?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Enough!

lines.speaker: King Thoros
lines.text: With our forces united, we should have the strength to take on these dragons and destroy them.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I take no joy in it, but war may be the only option here. If even half of what history says is true, the dragonkin are merciless.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Before this meeting, I thought I'd have a good old rummage around in our archives. I'm afraid the old bean is right. War is inevitable.

lines.speaker: Denulth
lines.text: With all due respect, how can we be sure that this is the work of a dragonkin? I trust [player name] as much as anyone, but what if there was a mistake?

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: It's no mistake. I saw it myself.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: They speak the truth.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Let me guess, you used your magic to work that one out?

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Careful, Brundt, I am not the enemy here.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: If we are agreed that the threat is real, may I suggest we get to work on solving it rather than bickering amongst ourselves.

lines.speaker: Denulth
lines.text: As much as I hate to agree with a White Knight, he's right. We need to work together if we are going to survive.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: So what do we do?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: The dragons are amassing at Ungael. We should take the fight to them and destroy the danger before it leaves that island!

lines.speaker: King Arthur
lines.text: That would keep the fight away from any innocents, but it would be a great risk.

lines.speaker: Sir Amik Varze
lines.text: Indeed. Boats do not do well against dragonfire.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: The citizens must take priority. If we have an opportunity to keep the danger away from them, we should take it.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Well said, fellow. Well said indeed.

lines.speaker: King Lathas/Thoros
lines.text: So we amass the fleets?

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: We do. Unless anyone has any complaints. [player name]? You've been very quiet throughout this. What are your thoughts?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a good plan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a bad plan.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: I fear we don't have a choice. The risks are great, but they were always going to be with a threat like this.

lines.speaker: Denulth
lines.text: He's right. This is the only option.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Then we are agreed. This room contains some of the most powerful people in the world. Heroes and rulers alike. We may be the only thing that stands in the way of annihilation.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: We may not all like each other, and some of us have a checkered history, but we will work together to destroy this enemy.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Good luck to us all.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You should join us for this fight, [Fremennik name]. Torfinn will take you to the fleet when you are ready.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Could I have a word with you outside, [player name]? There's something we need to discuss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Bob.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. We need to talk about Zorgoth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about him?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: He is the real threat here. You can kill as many dragons as you want in this battle, but it won't be over until Zorgoth himself is dealt with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a plan?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Dragonkin are not easy to kill. In fact, they are immune to most weapons. But Zorgoth made it clear that Galvek is the key to his plan. The only reason he has this army is because of that dragon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Galvek must die?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Yes. Without Galvek, Zorgoth has nothing. Removing his army is the only chance of stopping him. It must be done, no matter the cost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That won't be easy.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: It won't, but I have faith in you, [player name]. Now you'd better head to the fleet. I'll see you in Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're coming with us?

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I am. I can't fight any more, but I know a lot about the dragonkin. I might be able to use that knowledge to help. Besides, much of this is my fault, so it's only right that I help fix it. Now let's get going.

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: Good day to you, [Fremennik name]. Do you want to go to the fleet?

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: I'm not meant to be talking to you [Fremennik name]. I guess I can still take you to the fleet though if you want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Alert!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any of my items?

lines.speaker: Torfinn
lines.text: I'm afraid not [Fremennik name]. If I did have any of your items, but you died before collecting them, they'll now be lost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: I can see the island! We're almost there!

lines.speaker: Koschei the Deathless
lines.text: Incoming!

lines.speaker: Koschei the Deathless
lines.text: Time to wake up, [Fremennik name]. You have dragons to slay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh... what happened?

lines.speaker: Koschei the Deathless
lines.text: Dragons happened, that's what.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did everyone else go?

lines.speaker: Koschei the Deathless
lines.text: They went on ahead. You should catch up. I'll cover the rear.

sections.title: Talking to Bob on Varrock's grand ship

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: No time to talk, [player name]. Get out there and kill those dragons.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: [player name]! Thank goodness you're here. We've got ourselves into quite the situation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do? We're never going to make it to the island now.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: We'll have to worry about that later. For now, you'll need to take out these dragons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do my best.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Well look who it is. I see you're still alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not easy to kill.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: I doubt that.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: As for you, Robert...

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: You should have stayed at home. You're not a warrior anymore.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: Maybe not, but I'm right where I need to be.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: How touching.

lines.speaker: Bob
lines.text: I'm sorry, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Galvek!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bob!

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: See how the mighty fall.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You will pay for this!

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: We shall see...

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Why do you persist? Can't you see you've already lost?

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Your precious hero is gone! Your friends have abandoned you!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Don't be so sure.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Enough of this! You've had your fun! But that fun is at an end!

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: You will now face the full might of my power!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: We've got your back, [player name]. Now you show that dragon what for!

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Yes, come on, Dragon Slayer. Let's see what you've got.

lines.speaker: Zorgoth
lines.text: Nooooooooooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was for Bob!

lines.speaker: Jardric
lines.text: A true warrior.

lines.speaker: Oneiromancer
lines.text: Excellent work!

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good show old bean!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Splendid!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Great work, [Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Achietties
lines.text: You did it!

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Greetings adventurer. Welcome to the Myths' Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about my quest.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: I hear we have a hero in our midst. You have faced one of the greatest threats this world has ever seen and come out victorious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not without losses though.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Indeed. We will ensure their sacrifice is never forgotten.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: All that remains is to ensure that history will forever remember this day. Now, tell me everything.

lines.speaker: Alec Kincade
lines.text: Thank you friend. You have helped us to fill our Library of Knowledge. The secrets that you have uncovered are worth more than any coin. It is an honour to welcome you into the Myths' Guild.

lines.speaker: Sakirth
lines.text: Zorgoth?

lines.speaker: Sithaph
lines.text: Dead.

lines.speaker: Strisath
lines.text: Then we are free at last!

lines.speaker: Sakirth
lines.text: And a strength grows within us!

lines.speaker: Sithaph
lines.text: The Stone has been uncovered. The mortals try to claim its power.

lines.speaker: Sakirth
lines.text: Then let us bring terror and pain to them!

lines.speaker: Strisath
lines.text: Yes! The dragonkin will rise once more!
